questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Through point $O$ a straight line $L_1$ is drawn to cut two given straight lines $3 x+4 y-5=0$ and $x+2 y$ $-3=0$ at the points $\mathrm{L} \& \mathrm{M}$, where O is origin. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The equation of line $L_1$ if $O L: O M$ is equal to $5: 3$ is -
questions.solution:
Let equation of straight line through O is
$\frac{x}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y}{\sin \theta}=r$
Let $\mathrm{OL}=\mathrm{r}_1$ and $\mathrm{OM}=\mathrm{r}_2$, then the coordinates of $L$ and $M$ are given by $\mathrm{L}\left(\mathrm{r}_1 \cos \theta, \mathrm{r}_1 \sin \theta\right)$ and $\mathrm{m}\left(\mathrm{r}_2 \cos \theta, \mathrm{r}_2 \sin \theta\right)$. Let $N(h, k)$ be the variable point such that $O N$ $=r_3$, then $h=r_3 \cos \theta, k=r_3 \sin \theta$, since $L$ and $M$ lie on $3 x+4 y-5=0$ and $x+2 y-3=0$ so $r_1(3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta)=5$ and $r_2(\cos \theta+2 \sin \theta)=3$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_3}=\frac{5}{3 \mathrm{~h}+4 \mathrm{k}} \quad$ and $\quad \frac{\mathrm{r}_2}{\mathrm{r}_3}=\frac{3}{\mathrm{~h}+2 \mathrm{k}}$

$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Now } \frac{r_1}{r_2}=\frac{5}{3} \\
& \frac{5}{(3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta)} \times \frac{(\cos \theta+2 \sin \theta)}{3}=\frac{5}{3} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \cos \theta+2 \sin \theta=3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 \cos \theta+2 \sin \theta=0 \Rightarrow \tan \theta=-1 \\
& \therefore \quad \text { equation of } L_1 \text { will be } x+y=0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Straight lines $3 x+4 y=5$ and $4 x-3 y=15$ intersect at $A$. Points $B$ and $C$ are chosen on these lines such that $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{AC} .(1,2)$ is a point on the line BC . Distance of BC from A is less than 2 . On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ is -
questions.solution:
Line BC will be parallel to angle bisectors of angle $\mathrm{A}(3,-1)$ so $\quad \frac{3 x+4 y-5}{5}= \pm \frac{4 x-3 y-15}{5}$ $\Rightarrow$ by +ve sign $\quad x-7 y-10=0 \quad \ldots \ldots . B_1$ -ve sign $\quad 7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}-20=0 \quad \ldots \ldots . \mathrm{B}_2$ So $B C$ can be $x-7 y=\lambda_1 \Rightarrow \lambda_1=-13$ or $7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=\lambda_2 \Rightarrow \lambda_2=9$ so $x-7 y=-13$ or $7 x+y=9$ Distance of $x-7 y=-13$ from $A(3,-1)$ is $\frac{23}{\sqrt{50}}\gt2$ and distance of $7 x+y=9$ from $\mathrm{A}(3,-1)$ is $\frac{11}{5 \sqrt{2}} \lt 1$ so BC is $7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=9$
$\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ is right isosceles triangle so area of $\triangle A B C$ $=\frac{1}{2} 2 \lambda \cdot \lambda=\lambda^2=\left(\frac{11}{5 \sqrt{2}}\right)^2=\frac{121}{50}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Straight lines $3 x+4 y=5$ and $4 x-3 y=15$ intersect at $A$. Points $B$ and $C$ are chosen on these lines such that $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{AC} .(1,2)$ is a point on the line BC . Distance of BC from A is less than 2 . On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Circumcentre of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ is -
questions.solution:
Line BC will be parallel to angle bisectors of angle $\mathrm{A}(3,-1)$ so $\quad \frac{3 x+4 y-5}{5}= \pm \frac{4 x-3 y-15}{5}$ $\Rightarrow$ by +ve sign $\quad x-7 y-10=0 \quad \ldots \ldots . B_1$ -ve sign $\quad 7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}-20=0 \quad \ldots \ldots . \mathrm{B}_2$ So $B C$ can be $x-7 y=\lambda_1 \Rightarrow \lambda_1=-13$ or $7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=\lambda_2 \Rightarrow \lambda_2=9$ so $x-7 y=-13$ or $7 x+y=9$ Distance of $x-7 y=-13$ from $A(3,-1)$ is $\frac{23}{\sqrt{50}}\gt2$ and distance of $7 x+y=9$ from $\mathrm{A}(3,-1)$ is $\frac{11}{5 \sqrt{2}} \lt 1$ so BC is $7 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=9$
$\text { Now for 'B' } 3 x+4 y=5$ $7 x+y=9$ $-25 x=-31$ $\Rightarrow x=\frac{31}{25} \Rightarrow y=\frac{8}{25}$ $\mathrm{B}\left(\frac{31}{25}, \frac{8}{25}\right)$ Now for 'C' $4 x-3 y=15$ $7 x+y=9$ $25 x=42 \Rightarrow x=\frac{42}{25} \Rightarrow y=-\frac{69}{25}$ So $C\left(\frac{42}{25},-\frac{69}{25}\right)$ Circumcentre will be mid point of $\mathrm{BC}\left(\frac{73}{25},-\frac{61}{25}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Square $A B C D$ has vertices $A(1,4), B(5,4), C(5,8)$ and $D(1,8)$ from a point $P$ outside the square, a vertex of square is said to be visible if it can be connected to $P$ by a straight line that does not pass through square. Thus from any point $P$, outside either two or three of the vertices of the square are visible. The visible area of $P$ is the area of one triangle or the sum of the area of the two triangle formed by joining $P$ to the two or three visible vertices of the squareQuestion: Visible area of the point $P$ with coordinate $(2,-6)$ in square unit is
questions.solution:
The visible are of $P(2,-6)$ is the area of $\triangle A B P \triangle A B P$ has base $A B$ of length 4 and its height is the distance from $A B$ to point $P$. Which is 10 , since $A B$ is parallel to $x$-axis.
Thus area $=\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{b} \times \mathrm{h}=20$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: A straight line passing through $O(0,0)$ cuts the lines $x=\alpha, y=\beta$ and $x+y=8$ at $A, B$ and $C$ respectively such that $O A \cdot O B \cdot O C=48 \sqrt{2}$ and $f(\alpha, \beta) \leq 0$. Where $f(x, y)=\left|\frac{y}{x}-\frac{3}{2}\right|+(3 x-2 y)^6+\sqrt{e x+2 y-2 e-6}$Question: The point of intersection of lines $x=\alpha$ and $y=\beta$ is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Through point $O$ a straight line $L_1$ is drawn to cut two given straight lines $3 x+4 y-5=0$ and $x+2 y$ $-3=0$ at the points $\mathrm{L} \& \mathrm{M}$, where O is origin. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The equation of the angle bisector between two given lines which will contain the point $(2,3)$ is -
questions.solution:
Let equation of straight line through O is
$\frac{x}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y}{\sin \theta}=r$
Let $\mathrm{OL}=\mathrm{r}_1$ and $\mathrm{OM}=\mathrm{r}_2$, then the coordinates of $L$ and $M$ are given by $\mathrm{L}\left(\mathrm{r}_1 \cos \theta, \mathrm{r}_1 \sin \theta\right)$ and $\mathrm{m}\left(\mathrm{r}_2 \cos \theta, \mathrm{r}_2 \sin \theta\right)$. Let $N(h, k)$ be the variable point such that $O N$ $=r_3$, then $h=r_3 \cos \theta, k=r_3 \sin \theta$, since $L$ and $M$ lie on $3 x+4 y-5=0$ and $x+2 y-3=0$ so $r_1(3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta)=5$ and $r_2(\cos \theta+2 \sin \theta)=3$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_3}=\frac{5}{3 \mathrm{~h}+4 \mathrm{k}} \quad$ and $\quad \frac{\mathrm{r}_2}{\mathrm{r}_3}=\frac{3}{\mathrm{~h}+2 \mathrm{k}}$

$\frac{3 x+4 y-5}{5}= \pm \frac{x+2 y-3}{\sqrt{5}}$

Now $3(2)+4(3)-5\gt0$ and $2+2(3)-3\gt0$
so ' + ' sign bisector will contain the point $(2,3)$
$\begin{aligned}
& 3 x+4 y-5=\sqrt{5} x+2 \sqrt{5} y-3 \sqrt{5} \\
& (3-\sqrt{5}) x+2(2-\sqrt{5}) y+\sqrt{5}(3-\sqrt{5})=0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A straight line passing through $O(0,0)$ cuts the lines $x=\alpha, y=\beta$ and $x+y=8$ at $A, B$ and $C$ respectively such that $O A \cdot O B \cdot O C=48 \sqrt{2}$ and $f(\alpha, \beta) \leq 0$. Where $f(x, y)=\left|\frac{y}{x}-\frac{3}{2}\right|+(3 x-2 y)^6+\sqrt{e x+2 y-2 e-6}$Question: $\mathrm{OA}+\mathrm{OB}+\mathrm{OC}$ equals
questions.solution:
Let equation of straight line $\frac{x}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y}{\sin \theta}=r$
Let $O A=r_1, O B=r_2, O C=r_3$ then $A\left(r_1 \cos \theta, r_1 \sin \theta\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & B\left(r_2 \cos \theta, r_2 \sin \theta\right) \\ & C\left(r_3 \cos \theta, r_3 \sin \theta\right)\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \mathrm{A}$ lies on $\mathrm{x}=2, \mathrm{r}_1 \cos \theta=2$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{r}_1=\frac{2}{\cos \theta}$      .....(1)
$B$ lies on $y=3, r_2 \sin \theta=3, r_2=\frac{3}{\sin \theta}$    ....(2)
and $C$ lines on $x+y=8$
$r_3 \cos \theta+r_3 \sin \theta=8$
$\therefore r_1 r_2 r_3=48 \sqrt{2}$
$\left(\frac{2}{\cos \theta}\right)\left(\frac{3}{\sin \theta}\right)\left(\frac{8}{\sin \theta+\cos \theta}\right)=48 \sqrt{2}$
$\sqrt{2} \cos \theta \sin \theta(\cos \theta+\sin \theta)=1$
$\sin 2 \theta \sin \left(\theta, \frac{\pi}{4}\right)=1$
$\therefore$ Both $\sin 2 \theta$ and $\sin \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$ should be equal to 1 , so $\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}$ then $r_1=2 \sqrt{2}, r_2=3 \sqrt{2}$.
$r_3=\frac{8}{\sqrt{2}}=4 \sqrt{2}$
Hence, $O A+O B+O C=r_1+r_2+r_3=9 \sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $\mathrm{B}_1 \equiv 3 \mathrm{x}+4 \mathrm{y}-10=0$ and $\mathrm{B}_2 \equiv 4 \mathrm{x}-3 \mathrm{y}-5=0$ are bisectors of angle between lines $\mathrm{L}_1=0$ and $L_2=0$. If $L_1$ passes through origin and $L_2=0$ meets the curve $y^2=4 a x$ at $A$ and $B$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: If AB subtends $90^{\circ}$ at origin then a is equal to -
questions.solution:
Joint equation of the lines joining $\mathrm{A} \& \mathrm{~B}$ to origin is
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{y}^2-4 \mathrm{ax}\left(\frac{11 \mathrm{x}-20 \mathrm{y}}{20}\right)=0 \\
& 20 \mathrm{y}^2-44 \mathrm{ax}^2+80 \mathrm{axy}=0
\end{aligned}$

As AB subtends $90^{\circ}$ at origin. So $20-44 \mathrm{a}=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{a}=\frac{5}{11}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $\mathrm{B}_1 \equiv 3 \mathrm{x}+4 \mathrm{y}-10=0$ and $\mathrm{B}_2 \equiv 4 \mathrm{x}-3 \mathrm{y}-5=0$ are bisectors of angle between lines $\mathrm{L}_1=0$ and $L_2=0$. If $L_1$ passes through origin and $L_2=0$ meets the curve $y^2=4 a x$ at $A$ and $B$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Equation of line $L_1=0$ is -
questions.solution:
Clearly $L_1$ will pass through intersection point of $B_1 \& B_2$. So its equation will be
$(3 x+4 y-10)+\lambda(4 x-3 y-5)=0$
as $L_1$ passes through origin so $\lambda=-2$ hence equation of $L_1$
$\begin{aligned}
& -5 x+10 y=0 \\
& x=2 y
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider $\triangle A B C$ whose vertices are $A \equiv(m, n), B \equiv(1,2), C \equiv(2,3)$ and vertex 'A' lies on the line $2 x-y+3=0$ where $m, n \in N$ with $m^2+n^2=90$. Let area of $\triangle A B C$ be $S$ such that $[S]=2$, where $[x]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $x$.Question: If the equation of side $A C$ of $\Delta A B C$ is $a x+b y=9$, then $(a+b)$ equals
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { As } A(m, n) \text { lies on } y=2 x+3 \\ & \Rightarrow n=2 m+3 \\ & \therefore \quad A=(m, 2 m+3)\end{aligned}$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\mathrm{m} & 2 \mathrm{~m}+3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2|=\mathrm{S}$ (Given)
$\begin{aligned} & \text { But }[\mathrm{S}]=2 \Rightarrow 2 \leq \mathrm{S} \lt 3 \\ & \text { i.e., } 2 \leq \frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2| \lt 3\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow 4 \leq|m+2| \lt 6$ Now, $|\mathrm{m}+2| \lt 6 \Rightarrow-6 \lt \mathrm{m}+2 \lt 6$ $\Rightarrow-8 \lt m \lt 4$ $\ldots(1)$ and $|m+2| \geq 4 \Rightarrow m+2 \geq 4$ or $m+2 \leq-4$ $\Rightarrow m \geq 2$ or $m \leq-6$ $\therefore \quad$ From (i) and (ii), we get $-8 \lt m \leq-6 \text { or } 2 \leq m \angle 4$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{m}=-7,-6,2,3$ But $m \in N$, So, $m \neq-7,-6$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad m=2,3 \\ & \text { Also } m=2 \Rightarrow n=2 m+3=2(2)+3=7 \\ & \therefore \quad A(2,7)\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \quad \text { But } \mathrm{m}^2+\mathrm{n}^2=90 \text { and } 2^2+7^2 \neq 90 \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{~m} \neq 2 \\ & \text { Now } \mathrm{m}=3 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=2 \mathrm{~m}+3=2(3)+3=9\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad \mathrm{A}(3,9)$ Also $3^2+9^2=9+81=90$ Hence vertex 'A' of $\triangle A B C$ will be $(3,9)$ (i) Slope of $\mathrm{AC}=\frac{9-3}{3-2}=\frac{6}{1}=6$ $\therefore \quad$ Equation of $A C$ is $(y-3)=6(x-2)$
$\Rightarrow 6 x-y=9$ But we have equation of $A C$ is $a x+b=9$ $\therefore \quad a=6, b=-1$ $\Rightarrow a+b=5$ (ii) Clearly $R(\alpha, \beta)$ is centroid of $\triangle A B C$ $\therefore \quad R(\alpha, \beta)=\left(\frac{3+1+2}{3}, \frac{9+2+3}{3}\right)=\left(2, \frac{14}{3}\right)$ $\Rightarrow \alpha=2$ and $\beta=\frac{14}{3}$ Hence $2 \alpha+3 \beta=2(2)+3\left(\frac{14}{3}\right)=4+14=18$ (iii) We know that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. $\Rightarrow$ Midpoint of $A C=$ Midpoint of $B D$ $\left(\frac{3+2}{2}, \frac{9+3}{2}\right)=\left(\frac{r+1}{2}, \frac{s+2}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{r+1}{2}=\frac{5}{2} \\ & \therefore r=4\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } \frac{9+3}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{s}+2}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~s}=10\end{aligned}$ Hence $r=4, s=10$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider $\triangle A B C$ whose vertices are $A \equiv(m, n), B \equiv(1,2), C \equiv(2,3)$ and vertex 'A' lies on the line $2 x-y+3=0$ where $m, n \in N$ with $m^2+n^2=90$. Let area of $\triangle A B C$ be $S$ such that $[S]=2$, where $[x]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $x$.Question: If the point $R(\alpha, \beta)$ lies inside the $\triangle A B C$ is such that the $\triangle A B R, \triangle B C R$ and $\triangle C A R$ are of equal area, then ( $2 \alpha$ $+3 \beta$ ) equals
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { As } A(m, n) \text { lies on } y=2 x+3 \\ & \Rightarrow n=2 m+3 \\ & \therefore \quad A=(m, 2 m+3)\end{aligned}$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\mathrm{m} & 2 \mathrm{~m}+3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2|=\mathrm{S}$ (Given)
$\begin{aligned} & \text { But }[\mathrm{S}]=2 \Rightarrow 2 \leq \mathrm{S} \lt 3 \\ & \text { i.e., } 2 \leq \frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2| \lt 3\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow 4 \leq|m+2| \lt 6$ Now, $|\mathrm{m}+2| \lt 6 \Rightarrow-6 \lt \mathrm{m}+2 \lt 6$ $\Rightarrow-8 \lt m \lt 4$ $\ldots(1)$ and $|m+2| \geq 4 \Rightarrow m+2 \geq 4$ or $m+2 \leq-4$ $\Rightarrow m \geq 2$ or $m \leq-6$ $\therefore \quad$ From (i) and (ii), we get $-8 \lt m \leq-6 \text { or } 2 \leq m \angle 4$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{m}=-7,-6,2,3$ But $m \in N$, So, $m \neq-7,-6$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad m=2,3 \\ & \text { Also } m=2 \Rightarrow n=2 m+3=2(2)+3=7 \\ & \therefore \quad A(2,7)\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \quad \text { But } \mathrm{m}^2+\mathrm{n}^2=90 \text { and } 2^2+7^2 \neq 90 \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{~m} \neq 2 \\ & \text { Now } \mathrm{m}=3 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=2 \mathrm{~m}+3=2(3)+3=9\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad \mathrm{A}(3,9)$ Also $3^2+9^2=9+81=90$ Hence vertex 'A' of $\triangle A B C$ will be $(3,9)$ (i) Slope of $\mathrm{AC}=\frac{9-3}{3-2}=\frac{6}{1}=6$ $\therefore \quad$ Equation of $A C$ is $(y-3)=6(x-2)$
$\Rightarrow 6 x-y=9$ But we have equation of $A C$ is $a x+b=9$ $\therefore \quad a=6, b=-1$ $\Rightarrow a+b=5$ (ii) Clearly $R(\alpha, \beta)$ is centroid of $\triangle A B C$ $\therefore \quad R(\alpha, \beta)=\left(\frac{3+1+2}{3}, \frac{9+2+3}{3}\right)=\left(2, \frac{14}{3}\right)$ $\Rightarrow \alpha=2$ and $\beta=\frac{14}{3}$ Hence $2 \alpha+3 \beta=2(2)+3\left(\frac{14}{3}\right)=4+14=18$ (iii) We know that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. $\Rightarrow$ Midpoint of $A C=$ Midpoint of $B D$ $\left(\frac{3+2}{2}, \frac{9+3}{2}\right)=\left(\frac{r+1}{2}, \frac{s+2}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{r+1}{2}=\frac{5}{2} \\ & \therefore r=4\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } \frac{9+3}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{s}+2}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~s}=10\end{aligned}$ Hence $r=4, s=10$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider $\triangle A B C$ whose vertices are $A \equiv(m, n), B \equiv(1,2), C \equiv(2,3)$ and vertex 'A' lies on the line $2 x-y+3=0$ where $m, n \in N$ with $m^2+n^2=90$. Let area of $\triangle A B C$ be $S$ such that $[S]=2$, where $[x]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $x$.Question: If $A \equiv(m, n), B \equiv(1,2), C \equiv(2,3)$ and $D \equiv(r, s)$ are the vertices of a parallelogram $A B C D$, then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { As } A(m, n) \text { lies on } y=2 x+3 \\ & \Rightarrow n=2 m+3 \\ & \therefore \quad A=(m, 2 m+3)\end{aligned}$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\mathrm{m} & 2 \mathrm{~m}+3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2|=\mathrm{S}$ (Given)
$\begin{aligned} & \text { But }[\mathrm{S}]=2 \Rightarrow 2 \leq \mathrm{S} \lt 3 \\ & \text { i.e., } 2 \leq \frac{1}{2}|\mathrm{~m}+2| \lt 3\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow 4 \leq|m+2| \lt 6$ Now, $|\mathrm{m}+2| \lt 6 \Rightarrow-6 \lt \mathrm{m}+2 \lt 6$ $\Rightarrow-8 \lt m \lt 4$ $\ldots(1)$ and $|m+2| \geq 4 \Rightarrow m+2 \geq 4$ or $m+2 \leq-4$ $\Rightarrow m \geq 2$ or $m \leq-6$ $\therefore \quad$ From (i) and (ii), we get $-8 \lt m \leq-6 \text { or } 2 \leq m \angle 4$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{m}=-7,-6,2,3$ But $m \in N$, So, $m \neq-7,-6$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad m=2,3 \\ & \text { Also } m=2 \Rightarrow n=2 m+3=2(2)+3=7 \\ & \therefore \quad A(2,7)\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \quad \text { But } \mathrm{m}^2+\mathrm{n}^2=90 \text { and } 2^2+7^2 \neq 90 \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{~m} \neq 2 \\ & \text { Now } \mathrm{m}=3 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=2 \mathrm{~m}+3=2(3)+3=9\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad \mathrm{A}(3,9)$ Also $3^2+9^2=9+81=90$ Hence vertex 'A' of $\triangle A B C$ will be $(3,9)$ (i) Slope of $\mathrm{AC}=\frac{9-3}{3-2}=\frac{6}{1}=6$ $\therefore \quad$ Equation of $A C$ is $(y-3)=6(x-2)$
$\Rightarrow 6 x-y=9$ But we have equation of $A C$ is $a x+b=9$ $\therefore \quad a=6, b=-1$ $\Rightarrow a+b=5$ (ii) Clearly $R(\alpha, \beta)$ is centroid of $\triangle A B C$ $\therefore \quad R(\alpha, \beta)=\left(\frac{3+1+2}{3}, \frac{9+2+3}{3}\right)=\left(2, \frac{14}{3}\right)$ $\Rightarrow \alpha=2$ and $\beta=\frac{14}{3}$ Hence $2 \alpha+3 \beta=2(2)+3\left(\frac{14}{3}\right)=4+14=18$ (iii) We know that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. $\Rightarrow$ Midpoint of $A C=$ Midpoint of $B D$ $\left(\frac{3+2}{2}, \frac{9+3}{2}\right)=\left(\frac{r+1}{2}, \frac{s+2}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{r+1}{2}=\frac{5}{2} \\ & \therefore r=4\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } \frac{9+3}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{s}+2}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~s}=10\end{aligned}$ Hence $r=4, s=10$

questions.q: Paragraph: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } f(a, b)=\sqrt{49+a^2-7 \sqrt{2} a}+\sqrt{b^2+50-10 b}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-\sqrt{2} a b}\left(a, b \in R^{+}\right) \\ & g(a, b)=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-2 a+1}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-2 a+1}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-6 a-8 b+25} \\ & (a, b \in R) \text { and } h(a)=\left|\sqrt{a^2+4 a+5}-\sqrt{a^2+2 a+5}\right|(a \in R)\end{aligned}$Question: Least value of $(a, b)$ :
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(a, b)=\sqrt{\frac{a^2}{2}+\frac{a^2}{2}-7 \sqrt{2} a+49}+\sqrt{(b-5)^2+5^2}+\sqrt{\frac{a^2}{2}+\frac{a^2}{2}+b^2-\sqrt{2} a b c} \\ & f(a, b)=\underbrace{\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}-7\right)^2}}_{P Q}+\underbrace{\sqrt{(b-5)^2+5^2}}_{R S}+\underbrace{\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}-b\right)^2}}_{R Q} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
Image of $R$ is $R^{\prime}$ in $y=x$ $$ Q R=Q R^{\prime} $$ Also $S R=S R^{\prime}$ and image of $S$ is $S^{\prime}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & R^{\prime} S=R^{\prime} S^{\prime} \\ & \therefore \quad P Q+Q R^{\prime}+R^{\prime} S^{\prime} \geq P S^{\prime} \\ & \therefore \quad S R=S R^{\prime}=S^{\prime} R^{\prime} \end{aligned} $$ Sum of 3 sides of quad. $>4$ th side Min. value if $P S^{\prime}=\sqrt{144+25}=\sqrt{169}=13$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } f(a, b)=\sqrt{49+a^2-7 \sqrt{2} a}+\sqrt{b^2+50-10 b}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-\sqrt{2} a b}\left(a, b \in R^{+}\right) \\ & g(a, b)=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-2 a+1}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-2 a+1}+\sqrt{a^2+b^2-6 a-8 b+25} \\ & (a, b \in R) \text { and } h(a)=\left|\sqrt{a^2+4 a+5}-\sqrt{a^2+2 a+5}\right|(a \in R)\end{aligned}$Question: The least value of $g(a, b)$ is equal to $m$ and the greatest value of $h(a)$ is $n$ at $a=\alpha$ then $m+n+\alpha$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(a, b)=\sqrt{\frac{a^2}{2}+\frac{a^2}{2}-7 \sqrt{2} a+49}+\sqrt{(b-5)^2+5^2}+\sqrt{\frac{a^2}{2}+\frac{a^2}{2}+b^2-\sqrt{2} a b c} \\ & f(a, b)=\underbrace{\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}-7\right)^2}}_{P Q}+\underbrace{\sqrt{(b-5)^2+5^2}}_{R S}+\underbrace{\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}-b\right)^2}}_{R Q} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
Image of $R$ is $R^{\prime}$ in $y=x$ $$ Q R=Q R^{\prime} $$ Also $S R=S R^{\prime}$ and image of $S$ is $S^{\prime}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & R^{\prime} S=R^{\prime} S^{\prime} \\ & \therefore \quad P Q+Q R^{\prime}+R^{\prime} S^{\prime} \geq P S^{\prime} \\ & \therefore \quad S R=S R^{\prime}=S^{\prime} R^{\prime} \end{aligned} $$ Sum of 3 sides of quad. $>4$ th side Min. value if $P S^{\prime}=\sqrt{144+25}=\sqrt{169}=13$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is the image (reflection) of the point $(x, y)$ in the line $y=m x$, then $\mathrm{x}_1=\frac{1-\mathrm{m}^2}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{x}+\frac{2 \mathrm{~m}}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{y}$ and $\mathrm{y}_1=\frac{2 \mathrm{~m}}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{m}^2-1}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{y}$ $\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x_1 \\ y_1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\frac{1-m^2}{1+m^2} & \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} \\ \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} & \frac{m^2-1}{1+m^2}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right] \Rightarrow X_1=A X \Rightarrow X=A^{-1} X_1 \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\frac{1-m^2}{1+m^2} & \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} \\ \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} & \frac{m^2-1}{1+m^2}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}x_1 \\ y_1\end{array}\right],\left(\right.$ since $\left.A^2=I\right)$Question: A ray moving along the line $p x+q y=1$, after striking a mirror placed along the line $y=3$, is reflected along the line
questions.solution:
Shift the origin to the point $(0,3)$ so that any point $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ on the reflected line is given by
$$
\begin{aligned}
& {\left[\begin{array}{c}
x_1 \\
y_1-3
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}
1 & 0 \\
0 & -1
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
\frac{1-q(h+3)}{p} \\
h
\end{array}\right](\text { since } m=0)} \\
& \Rightarrow x_1=\frac{1-q(h+3)}{p}, y_1-3=-h \Rightarrow p x_1=q y_1+1-6 q
\end{aligned}
$$
and hence the reflected line is $p x-q y=1-6 q$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is the image (reflection) of the point $(x, y)$ in the line $y=m x$, then $\mathrm{x}_1=\frac{1-\mathrm{m}^2}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{x}+\frac{2 \mathrm{~m}}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{y}$ and $\mathrm{y}_1=\frac{2 \mathrm{~m}}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{m}^2-1}{1+\mathrm{m}^2} \mathrm{y}$ $\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x_1 \\ y_1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\frac{1-m^2}{1+m^2} & \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} \\ \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} & \frac{m^2-1}{1+m^2}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right] \Rightarrow X_1=A X \Rightarrow X=A^{-1} X_1 \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\frac{1-m^2}{1+m^2} & \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} \\ \frac{2 m}{1+m^2} & \frac{m^2-1}{1+m^2}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}x_1 \\ y_1\end{array}\right],\left(\right.$ since $\left.A^2=I\right)$Question: The image of the parabola $y^2=x$ in the line $x+y+4=0$ is
questions.solution:
Any point on the parabola is $\left(t^2, t\right)$. Shift the origin to $(-4,0)$ so that the line becomes $X+y=0$ and the point $\left(t^2\right.$, $\left.t\right)$ becomes $\left(4+t^2, t\right)$ where $X=x+4$. If $\left(X_1, y_1\right)$ is the image of $\left(4+t^2, t\right)$ in $x+y=0$, then $\left[\begin{array}{l}X_1 \\ y_1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}0 & -1 \\ -1 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}4+t^2 \\ t\end{array}\right]$
$\Rightarrow x_1=-t, y_1=-\left(4+t^2\right)$ and in the original coordinates $x_1=-4-t, y_1=-4-t^2$
$\Rightarrow$ the equation of image is $(x+4)^2=-(y+4)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
